title: Borrowers don't have right to personal hearing before banks classify accounts as fraud: Supreme Court
description: The Supreme Court recently held that borrowers are not entitled to a personal hearing before their loan accounts are classified as “fraud” under Reserve Bank of India (RBI) directions. [State Bank of India v. Amit Iron]A Bench of Justices JB Pardiwala and KV Viswanathan held "Rajesh Agarwal (supra) did not recognize any right in the borrower to a personal hearing by the banks before classifying their account as a fraud account."Justice JB Pardiwala and Justice KV ViswanathanThe case reached the
